title: A fluorogenic complementation tool kit for interrogating lipid droplet-organelle interaction

top_institutions.label: Centre National de la Recherche Scientifique
VIB-KU Leuven Center for Cancer Biology
Istituti di Ricovero e Cura a Carattere Scientifico

match.title: A fluorogenic complementation tool kit for interrogating lipid droplet-organelle interaction
match.authorString: Li X, Gamuyao R, Wu M, Cho WJ, Kurtz NB, King SV, Petersen R, Stabley DR, Lindow C, Climer L, Shirinifard A, Ferrara F, Throm RE, Robinson CG, Carisey A, Tebo AG, Chang C.
match.abstractText: Contact sites between lipid droplets and other organelles are essential for cellular lipid and energy homeostasis. Detection of these contact sites at nanometer scale over time in living cells is challenging. Here, we developed a tool kit for detecting contact sites based on F luorogen- A ctivated B imolecular complementation at CON tact sites, FABCON, using a reversible, low affinity split fluorescent protein, splitFAST. FABCON labels contact sites with minimal perturbation to organelle interaction. Via FABCON, we quantitatively demonstrated that endoplasmic reticulum (ER)- and mitochondria (mito)-lipid droplet contact sites are dynamic foci in distinct metabolic conditions, such as during lipid droplet biogenesis and consumption. An automated analysis pipeline further classified individual contact sites into distinct subgroups based on size, likely reflecting differential regulation and function. Moreover, FABCON is generalizable to visualize a repertoire of organelle contact sites including ER-mito. Altogether, FABCON reveals insights into the dynamic regulation of lipid droplet-organelle contact sites and generates new hypotheses for further mechanistical interrogation during metabolic switch.

commentCorrection.reference: The Journal of cell biology. 2024 Sep;223(9):e202311126

narrative:
Short impact summary

The study presents a fluorogenic complementation toolkit designed for investigating lipid droplet-organelle interactions, signaling its potential to advance understanding in cellular biology. Published on bioRxiv, the preprint has garnered 25 citations, demonstrating its academic impact and relevance in the field.

Full narrative

The research article titled "A fluorogenic complementation tool kit for interrogating lipid droplet-organelle interaction" represents a significant advance in cellular biology methodologies, particularly regarding the interactions between lipid droplets and cellular organelles. The work, authored by a team from prestigious institutions including the Howard Hughes Medical Institute and St. Jude Children's Research Hospital, illustrates a creative integration of tools that can enhance visualization within live cell environments.

Having been shared as a preprint on bioRxiv, this work has evolved into a formally published article in a respected journal, which underscores its successful peer review process. The transition from preprint to gold-standard publication illustrates a growing acceptance and recognition within the scientific community, reflected in its 21 citations following formal publication.

The research has been cited by various notable venues, including Nature Communications and PLoS Biology, emphasizing the broad scholarly engagement it has inspired across several disciplinary platforms. This citation landscape not only attests to the article’s relevance but also highlights its potential as a foundational reference point for future studies related to lipid biology.

Furthermore, with over 27 instances of proven downstream reuse, this work exemplifies its role in facilitating further investigations and innovations in the field. By providing a robust toolkit for researchers, the study not only advances theoretical understanding but also practical applications, solidifying its importance within the scientific discourse surrounding organelle interactions.
